D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Optical Activity of a Sugar Solution

send polarized light through sugar and watch its plane of vibration rotate

Chiral molecules see right- and left-circular light at slightly different speeds, so a linear input emerges rotated. The rotation grows with concentration and path, and falls with wavelength.

Set the sucrose concentration, the tube length, and the wavelength. The output polarization arrow rotates by β; shorter wavelengths rotate more (rotatory dispersion).
